conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 17 years old, his eligibility depends on whether he's in approved education or training. Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, college courses, or an approved training program)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in college or doing A-levels. He's actually started an apprenticeship recently - he's doing a training course to learn a trade.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask an important clarifying question about Alex's apprenticeship:

Is Alex doing an apprenticeship in England, or is it in Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's doing his apprenticeship in England. We live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I also need to confirm a few more details:

Do you live in the UK, and is Alex living with you at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. I live in Manchester and Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official guidance, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Although Alex is 17 years old and lives at home with you, the Child Benefit rules state that you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has recently started an apprenticeship in England, your entitlement to Child Benefit for him has ended.

This rule applies specifically to apprenticeships in England - even though he's under 20 and living with you, the apprenticeship makes him ineligible for Child Benefit purposes.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, you should contact the Child Benefit Office to inform them of this change in circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about this decision?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child 'starts an apprenticeship in England'. Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England, you are no longer eligible to receive Child Benefit for him, even though he is under 20 and lives at home with you.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 17-year-old son Alex. While Alex is under 20 and lives at home, he has recently started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, benefit payments stop when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child 'starts an apprenticeship in England'. Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England, you are no longer eligible to receive Child Benefit for him, even though he is under 20 and lives at home with you.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 17-year-old son Alex. While Alex is under 20 and lives at home, he has recently started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, benefit payments stop when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
